rulings.Case Title: DEBRA WITT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant The Regents of the University of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. George was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Cantley¿s deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. Govt. Code § 835 was not the only theory of liability alleged against defendant in plaintiff¿s amended complaint. To the extent that defendant made an alternative motion for summary adjudication, the motion is denied due to lack of compliance with summary adjudication rules requiring that each issue sought to be summarily adjudicated be separately identified and supported with specified purported undisputed facts. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HRUSKA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Crane Co.¿S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011, line 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary adjudication will be heard at 9:30 am in Dept. 504, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion is granted as to issue 1 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 2 and 3 (false representation and intentional tort). As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue of material fact whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objection to plaintiffs¿ supplemental interrogatory response is sustained and its objection to its responses to General Order interrogatories is overruled. As to false representation and intentional tort, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting those causes of action. Defendant failed to demonstrate that it propounded special discovery asking for all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiffs¿ false representation and intentional tort causes of action, or that any responses thereto were factually devoid. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011, line 2. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s demurrer will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 504, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s demurrer to the first, second, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action in plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ causes of action contain allegations sufficient to state the bases for their claims against defendant. This includes sufficient product identification under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, and sufficient allegations as to the false representation (Restatement 402-B) and intentional tort (Civil Code Sections 1708, 1709 and 1710) causes of action. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Based on defendant¿s representation to the court on August 24, defendant is not contesting this tentative ruling. Plaintiff to bring a proposed order to the hearing that conforms to this tentative ruling. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011, line 2. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s motion to strike will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 504, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion to strike plaintiffs¿ prayer for punitive damages is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The court did not consider plaintiffs¿ opposition. Pursuant to C.C.P. § 1005(b), plaintiffs¿ opposition was due by August 11. Plaintiffs did not file their opposition until August 19, only 3 court days before this hearing. Plaintiffs¿ opposition and attorney declaration failed to demonstrate any good cause for the court to consider the late-filed opposition. See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019). Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing that conforms to this tentative ruling. = (503/TLJ)
